sections.paragraph:
constr. and followed by לְ ( Exo 12:14 Num 29:12) חַג, with suff. חַגִּי m.

(1) a festival (from the root חָגַג), Exo 10:9 12:14 . עָשָׂה חָג, חַג חָג to keep a festival Levit. 23:39 Deu 16:10. In the Talmud κατʼ ἐξοχὴν, it is used of the feast of tabernacles, and so 2Ch 5:3 comp. 1Ki 8:2. [“So of the passover, Isa 30:29. Comp. Arabic حِجُ pilgrimage to Mecca.”]

(2) meton. a festival sacrifice, a victim, Psa 118:27, אִסְרוּ־חַג בַּעֲבֹתִים “bind the sacrifice with cords.” Exo 23:18, חֶלֶב חַגִּי “the fat of my sacrifice;” Mal 2:3. Compare מֹועֵד 2Ch 30:22.
